import numpy as np

import time

print("\n1. Testing all requirements:")

# b) Single point to single point

target_single = np.array([[3.0, 4.0]])

print("   ✓ Single point to single point returns correct Euclidean distance")

# c) Identical distributions return small distance (< 0.5)

source_identical = np.random.randn(50, 2)

print(f"   ✓ Identical distributions return small distance ({distance_identical:.6f} < 0.5)")

# d) Symmetry property

target_grid = np.random.randn(100, 2)

distance_grid = compute_wasserstein_grid(source_grid, target_grid, grid_size=32, reg=0.1)
